ab: ’arupacʉ’ʉra kita ca’ania nura, tia kara manʉng?
ch: W:我和您約明天下午好嗎?
en: Can I make an appointment with you tomorrow afternoon?

ab: tʉni, tia pon nanu kita ’arupacʉ’ʉra?
ch: M:可以，我們約哪裡見面?
en: Sure, where should we meet?

ab: tia kara manʉng na tarisinata? tia kim mapusikarʉ ’aningu.
ch: W:我們約學校好嗎?我們要錄影。
en: How about meeting at school? We need to shoot a video.

ab: mukusa kʉcʉ na Cakʉrʉ, matikuru ku Kanakanavu.
ch: M:那去聚會所好了，我會穿族服去。
en: Then let’s go to the community center. I’ll wear folk costume.

ab: noo tumavungavung sakura puraku mia manʉngʉcau, maritarʉ noo tumavungauvng cu ia, iavataa kasu tikirimi ’ikua.
ch: W: 我們部落的櫻花盛開很漂亮。等花開的時候你來找我吧!
en: The cherry blossoms in our tribe are beautiful when they bloom. Come find me when the flowers bloom!

ab: makasuin tia maritarʉ na kana’iara, tia kara rovana curu!
ch: M: 那要等到春天，太晚了吧!
en: That would have to wait until spring. It’s too late, isn’t it?

ab: makasuin ivataa noo Mikongu.
ch: W: 那你米貢祭的時候來吧!
en: Then come during our Mikongu!

ab: manʉng! ’esi cu ku maritarʉ.
ch: M: 好啊!我很期待!
en: Alright! I’m looking forward to it!

ab: kesoni kasua, neni vanai tia aravari na puraku cumacʉvʉrʉ.
ch: W: 請問你為甚麼要搬回部落生活?
en: May I ask why you decided to move back to the tribe?

ab: tia ku pui’i tumatuturu sinatʉ.
ch: M: 我要回去教書。
en: I want to return to teach at the local school.

ab: meseni ka’anʉ na namanamarʉ tumatuturu sinatʉ?
ch: W: 為甚麼不在都市教書?
en: Why not teach in the city?

ab: vanai sii pacvʉpʉngʉ ku tanasʉn, pacʉpʉngʉ tia paso’e na puraku.
ch: M: 因為我懷念我的故鄉，也希望能回饋部落。
en: Because I miss my hometown and I also want to give back to the tribe.

ab: saihu kasu tumata’isi, neni tumatuturu kasua?
ch: M:你很會織布，是誰教你的?
en: You’re very skilled at weaving, who taught you?

ab: tamu nanakʉ maku, sua nguain ia, puraku mia mastaan saihu tumata’isi.
ch: W:是我的奶奶，她是我們部落最會織布的人。
en: It was my grandmother, she is the most skilled weaver in our tribe.

ab: miseni kasu pacʉpʉngʉ tia kiatuturu tumata’isi?
ch: M:你怎麼會想要學織布?
en: How did you develop an interest in learning weaving?

ab: mungka mia sua tumata’isi, pacʉpʉngʉ ku tia ’apacuvungu mungka isi.
ch: W:織布是我們的文化，我想要讓文化延續。
en: Weaving is a part of our culture, and I want to ensure its continuity.

ab: cucuruana mamanʉng imʉʉn pa’ici isi!
ch: M:這個小米酒真好喝。
en: This millet wine is delicious.

ab: iku tinakacicini kapa’ici.
ch: W:是我自己釀的喔!
en: I made it myself!

ab: sauhuana kasu, tia kara miaranau kamanʉngʉn?
ch: M:你真厲害，這要釀很久嗎?
en: You’re really talented. Does it take a long time to make?

ab: ka’anʉ, manasʉ cani tarasangara tʉni cu. tia pata’uruʉn parai mataa vʉra pociriʉn, tia pa usʉ’ʉn ti’ingi tamu pa’ici, ’apusikarʉn cu na pungana.
ch: W:不用，大約一周就好了。把糯米和白米先蒸熟，再加入麴粉一起拌勻，就可以裝到甕裡了。
en: No, it only takes about a week. After steaming the sticky rice and white rice, we mix it with yeast powder, and then it can be stored in a jar.
